que: What is the goal of responsive design in Flutter?
options: To make every screen look identical at every size
To adapt the UI to different screen sizes and constraints
To remove the need for layout widgets

que: Which Flutter API is commonly used to read screen size information?
Which widget helps respond to parent layout constraints?
Which practice is recommended for responsive design?
options: Assume phone layouts work on tablets unchanged
Put all layout logic in main()

que: Which widgets are often useful in responsive layouts?
What should you avoid in many responsive layouts?
options: Relying too heavily on fixed pixel sizes

que: Why might a Row overflow on smaller screens?
options: Because rigid child sizing may not fit the available width

que: What is a sensible design approach for responsive layouts?
options: Design for narrow screens first and scale up
